---
title: "SAT Score Distribution by Race, 2018–2025"
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    fig-width: 11
    fig-height: 9
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(purrr)
library(ggplot2)
```

```{r}
#| label: plot
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false

years <- 2018:2025
files <- sprintf("../scores/data/sat/%d.csv", years)

bucket_levels <- c("400-590", "600-790", "800-990",
                   "1000-1190", "1200-1390", "1400-1600")

race_levels <- c("Asian", "White", "Two or More Races",
                 "Hispanic/Latino", "Native Hawaiian/Other Pacific Islander",
                 "Black/African American", "American Indian/Alaska Native")

plot_df <- map_dfr(files, read_csv, show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  filter(score_range != "all", race %in% race_levels) |>
  mutate(score_range = factor(score_range, levels = bucket_levels),
         race        = factor(race,        levels = race_levels),
         year        = factor(year))

ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = score_range, y = percent, fill = year)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.85), width = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ race, ncol = 2) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "viridis", name = "Year") +
  labs(x = "Total score",
       y = "Percent of group",
       caption = "Source: College Board, SAT Suite Annual Reports 2018–2025") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "bottom")
```
